seoTitle: Add Expandable Sections in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add expandable, collapsible sections in Confluence in 2026 using the Expand macro to hide dense details behind a clean, scannable title.
intro: A wall of dense technical detail overwhelms readers before they get to the important parts. This guide shows how to add collapsible expand sections in Confluence in 2026 to keep pages scannable while still preserving detail.

steps.heading: Open the expand macro
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/expand` or `/collapse` and hit Enter to drop the interactive collapsible frame.

steps.heading: Title the section
steps.body: Click the header input strip to type a clear, descriptive title for what's hidden inside.

steps.heading: Add your content
steps.body: Move into the main container block to type detailed explanations or drop in structured data tables.

steps.heading: Set the default state
steps.body: Use the sidebar options drawer to toggle whether the section loads expanded or fully collapsed by default.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review your layout and click Publish to go live.

faq.question: Can I set an expand section to be open by default?
faq.answer: Yes, the macro's configuration lets you choose whether the block loads expanded or fully collapsed.

faq.question: Can I put a table inside an expandable section?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop complex bulleted lists, code blocks, or data tables inside the macro boundary.

faq.question: Does hiding content behind an expand block slow the page down?
faq.answer: No, collapsing heavy data arrays actually helps the page render faster and reduces scroll length.

faq.question: Are expand sections good for building an FAQ page?
faq.answer: Yes, layering multiple expandable rows together is a common way to build interactive FAQ hubs and technical spec summaries.
